# @sirrlock/node — Sirr Node.js SDK

TypeScript/JavaScript client for the Sirr ephemeral secret vault HTTP API.
Published to npm as `@sirrlock/node`. Zero production dependencies, native fetch.
ESM + CJS output. Node 18+.

## Install

npm install @sirrlock/node

## Usage

import { SirrClient } from '@sirrlock/node'

const client = new SirrClient({ server: 'http://localhost:39999', token: 'your-key' })

await client.push('DB_PASSWORD', 's3cr3t', { ttl: 3600, reads: 1 })
const value = await client.get('DB_PASSWORD')   // null if burned/expired
await client.delete('DB_PASSWORD')
const secrets = await client.list()
const all = await client.pullAll()              // { KEY: 'value', ... }
await client.withSecrets(async () => { /* process.env populated */ })
await client.prune()
await client.health()

## Errors

- get() returns null on 404 (burned/expired) — never throws for not-found
- All other non-2xx responses throw SirrError (has .status number)
- Constructor throws if token is empty

## Source

github.com/sirrlock/node
